message:
🌱 Add tikv: Full chunk-based computing in coprocessor mission (#761)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "helm-7956-helm-upgrade-fails-with-spec-clusterip-invalid-value-field-is-immutabl",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "helm: helm upgrade fails with spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: \"\": field is immutable",
+    "description": "helm upgrade fails with spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: \"\": field is immutable. This issue affects 97+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify helm troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your helm deployment:\n```bash\nhelm version\nhelm env\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review helm configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant helm configuration:\n```bash\nhelm env\nhelm repo list 2>/dev/null || echo \"No repositories configured\"\n```\nWhen issue helm upgrade, it shows errors like, (\"my-service\" change from \"clusterIP: None\" to \"type: LoadBalancer\" without field clusterIP)\n\n```\nError: UPGRADE FAILED: Service \"my-service\" is invalid: spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: \"\": field is"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for helm upgrade fails with spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: \"\":\u2026",
+        "description": "So https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/6378#issuecomment-557746499 is correct. Please read that before continuing on with this issue. If `clusterIP: \"\"` is set, Kubernetes will assign an IP. On the next Helm upgrade, if `clusterIP:\"\"` again, it will give the error above, because it appears _to\n```yaml\nError: UPGRADE FAILED: Service \"my-service\" is invalid: spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: \"\": field is immutable\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade helm to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade helm:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade helm helm/helm --namespace helm\n```\nVerify the upgrade:\n```bash\nhelm list -n helm\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm helm upgrade fails with spec.clusterIP: Invalid\u2026 is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm -n helm --tail=50 --since=5m\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "So https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/6378#issuecomment-557746499 is correct. Please read that before continuing on with this issue. If `clusterIP: \"\"` is set, Kubernetes will assign an IP. On the next Helm upgrade, if `clusterIP:\"\"` again, it will give the error above, because it appears _to Kubernetes_ that you are trying to reset the IP.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Error: UPGRADE FAILED: Service \"my-service\" is invalid: spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: \"\": field is immutable",
+        "Client: &version.Version{SemVer:\"v2.12.3\", GitCommit:\"eecf22f77df5f65c823aacd2dbd30ae6c65f186e\", GitTreeState:\"clean\"}\nServer: &version.Version{SemVer:\"v2.14.3\", GitCommit:\"0e7f3b6637f7af8fcfddb3d2941fcc7cbebb0085\", GitTreeState:\"clean\"}",
+        "Client Version: version.Info{Major:\"1\", Minor:\"16\", GitVersion:\"v1.16.0\", GitCommit:\"2bd9643cee5b3b3a5ecbd3af49d09018f0773c77\", GitTreeState:\"clean\", BuildDate:\"2019-09-18T14:36:53Z\", GoVersion:\"go1.12.9\", Compiler:\"gc\", Platform:\"linux/amd64\"}\nServer Version: version.Info{Major:\"1\", Minor:\"14+\", GitVersion:\"v1.14.10-gke.27\", GitCommit:\"145f9e21a4515947d6fb10819e5a336aff1b6959\", GitTreeState:\"clean\", BuildDate:\"2020-02-21T18:01:40Z\", GoVersion:\"go1.12.12b4\", Compiler:\"gc\", Platform:\"linux/amd64\"}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "helm",
+      "graduated",
+      "app-definition",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod",
+      "Service"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "expert",

+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/7956",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/helm/helm"
+    },
+    "reactions": 97,
+    "comments": 70,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-13T06:20:09.667Z",

@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "tikv-7724-full-chunk-based-computing-in-coprocessor",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "tikv: Full chunk-based computing in coprocessor",
+    "description": "Full chunk-based computing in coprocessor. Requested by 7+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current tikv deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your tiup --version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv\nhelm list -n tikv 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working tikv installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review tikv configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant tikv configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv\nkubectl get configmap -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=tikv\n```\nUsing [TiDB Chunk format](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/9627132db25e5cb560ba6ed89c2e39c32edb267a/util/chunk/chunk.go) in the `coprocessor` framework instead of `VectorValue`."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Full chunk-based computing in coprocessor",
+        "description": "### What problem does this PR solve?\n\nThis PR changes all vectorized functions to have signature like `do_something(a: Option<&Int>, b: Option<&Json>`.\n\nIn [RFC: Using chunk format in coprocessor framework](https://github.com/tikv/rfcs/pull/43/files), we proposed a `ChunkedVec` struct to support Chunk-based computing. We plan to:\n\n1. Migrate current co-processor framework to use interface compatible with `ChunkedVec`. \n\nThis is done by adding new functionalities on current `Vec<Option<T>>`. The\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv\nkubectl get events -n tikv --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Full chunk-based computing in coprocessor\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "### What problem does this PR solve?\n\nThis PR changes all vectorized functions to have signature like `do_something(a: Option<&Int>, b: Option<&Json>`.\n\nIn [RFC: Using chunk format in coprocessor framework](https://github.com/tikv/rfcs/pull/43/files), we proposed a `ChunkedVec` struct to support Chunk-based computing. We plan to:\n\n1.\n\nSee the source issue and linked PR for the complete implementation details.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "tikv",
+      "graduated",
+      "storage",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "tikv"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/7724",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/tikv/tikv",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/7979"
+    },
+    "reactions": 7,
+    "comments": 8,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "tikv-ctl",
+      "tiup"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running TiKV cluster (standalone or deployed on Kubernetes via TiDB Operator). TiUP and tikv-ctl are the primary CLI tools for managing TiKV."
+  },
+  "security": {
